verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Rey Cortiço at After work (16:30 start) looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The wind looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.windFit: NNW 15 km/h · onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 12 kJ, 10 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at mid tide also works on Belgas, but low tide is not a deal-breaker.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; exposed north spot probably too small.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
exposed north spot probably too small
